title: Antirhea viscosa
taxonomy: Antirhea viscosa is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family includes approximately 6000 to 9000 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Antirhea itself is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Antirhea viscosa is a species of flowering plant that belongs to this genus and family. The classification of Antirhea viscosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and helps scientists to better understand its characteristics and behaviors.
